concerns.text: The plan cites HuggingFace parquet files (e.g., `chy626/icecube_cup_w_distractors_new_0`) as the source for IceCube and Auger event data (2010–2020). These datasets are typically associated with the 'CUP' (Cosmic Ray Unsupervised Pretraining) challenge or specific ML benchmarks, often containing simulated or restricted subsets, not the full raw event streams required for a 10-year anisotropy study. The spec (FR-001) requires 'open-data portals' (IceCube/Auger official archives). Relying on these specific HF URLs risks using the wrong modality (simulated vs. real) or insufficient temporal coverage, rendering the anisotropy analysis invalid.

The plan assumes the HuggingFace parquet files contain 'timestamp, ra, dec, energy' for the full 2010–2020 range. Public HF datasets for IceCube/Auger are often small, simulated, or specific to a single year/event type. If these files lack the necessary 10-year temporal span or the specific high-energy event counts required for dipole fitting, the pipeline will fail to generate the required time-series (US-1). The plan must verify the specific dataset content matches the 10-year real-data requirement or switch to official open-data portals (e.g., IceCube Open Data, Auger Open Data).
